description_long: This kiosk consists of two adjacent rectangular booths separated by transparent partitions, each with a counter or desk inside. The structure is primarily composed of solid lower panels and clear upper panels, likely made of glass or acrylic, supported by a minimal black frame. The interior features simple, light brown counters or benches, providing a functional workspace for each booth. The overall design is open at the front, allowing easy access for users or customers, while the sides and rear are enclosed by transparent panels. The kiosk is compact and modular, suitable for installation on sidewalks, in parks, or within commercial centers. Its clean lines and neutral color palette give it a modern, utilitarian appearance.
description: A modern, dual-booth kiosk with transparent upper panels, solid lower panels, and integrated counters, designed for open public interaction.
description_view_0: Front view showing two adjacent booths with transparent partitions and visible counters inside.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the booths and the separation between the two workspaces.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the counters and the enclosed nature of the back panels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arrangement of the counters and the transparent partitions from a different angle.
attribute_roof_design: flat roof, constructed from the same transparent material as the upper panels, no overhangs or decorative features
